# Share Kit — Enterprise Windows Infrastructure Security

**Repo:** https://github.com/armourinfosec/Enterprise-Windows-Infrastructure-Security
**License:** CC BY 4.0 · **By:** Armour Infosec

Copy-paste blocks per platform. Facts used: 22 lab-driven modules; covers networking,
Windows Server admin & PowerShell, Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, DHCP, DFS, IIS,
FTP, proxy, VPN/remote access, security hardening, monitoring & logging, backup & recovery,
practical labs, enterprise projects. Lab: Windows Server 2019/2022/2025 + Windows 10/11 + Kali on 10.10.10.0/24.

---

## LinkedIn

🛡️ Enterprise Windows Infrastructure Security — open, hands-on study notes

We've published a free, practical knowledge base for building and securing enterprise Windows environments — from networking fundamentals all the way to Active Directory, core services, and monitoring.

22 modules covering:
• Networking fundamentals & lab setup
• Windows Server administration & PowerShell
• Active Directory (AD DS), Group Policy, DNS, DHCP, DFS
• IIS, FTP, proxy, and Remote Access / VPN
• Enterprise security hardening, monitoring & logging, backup & recovery
• Practical labs and end-to-end enterprise projects

Every module is lab-driven around a consistent environment (Windows Server 2019/2022/2025 + Windows 10/11 + Kali on 10.10.10.0/24). Licensed CC BY 4.0 — free to use and share with attribution.

🔗 https://github.com/armourinfosec/Enterprise-Windows-Infrastructure-Security

⭐ Star it, share it, and open an issue if you spot something to improve.

By Armour Infosec — offensive security, done responsibly.

#WindowsServer #ActiveDirectory #CyberSecurity #BlueTeam #InfoSec #SysAdmin #DevSecOps

### X thread (optional)
1/ We open-sourced our Enterprise Windows Infrastructure Security notes — 22 lab-driven modules, free under CC BY 4.0. 🧵
2/ Foundations: networking, lab setup & virtualization (VirtualBox / VMware / KVM-QEMU).
3/ Windows admin: Server management, OS administration, commands, PowerShell.
4/ Directory & services: Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, DHCP, DFS.
5/ App & access: IIS, FTP, proxy, Remote Access / VPN.
6/ Security & ops: hardening, monitoring & logging, backup & recovery, plus practical labs and enterprise projects.
7/ ⭐ Star + share: https://github.com/armourinfosec/Enterprise-Windows-Infrastructure-Security

## Reddit (e.g. r/sysadmin, r/cybersecurity, r/WGU, r/homelab)

**Title:** Free, open study notes: Enterprise Windows Infrastructure Security (22 lab-driven modules, CC BY 4.0)

**Body:**
Sharing an open knowledge base we put together for learning how to build and secure enterprise Windows environments. It's structured as 22 modules — networking, Windows Server admin & PowerShell, Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, DHCP, DFS, IIS, FTP, proxy, Remote Access/VPN, security hardening, monitoring & logging, backup & recovery, plus practical labs and full enterprise projects.

Everything is hands-on and built around a consistent lab (Windows Server 2019/2022/2025 + Windows 10/11 + Kali on 10.10.10.0/24). Licensed CC BY 4.0, so it's free to use, adapt, and share.

Repo: https://github.com/armourinfosec/Enterprise-Windows-Infrastructure-Security

Feedback, issues, and PRs welcome.

## Email / Newsletter

**Subject:** Free & open: Enterprise Windows Infrastructure Security (22 lab-driven modules)

Hi there,

We've just published a free, open-source knowledge base: **Enterprise Windows Infrastructure Security**.

It's a hands-on, 22-module guide to designing, building, administering, and securing enterprise Windows environments — from networking fundamentals through Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, DHCP, DFS, IIS, FTP, proxy, and Remote Access/VPN, into security hardening, monitoring & logging, and backup & recovery. It closes with practical labs and end-to-end enterprise projects.

Every module is lab-driven around a consistent environment (Windows Server 2019/2022/2025 + Windows 10/11 + Kali on 10.10.10.0/24), and the whole thing is licensed CC BY 4.0 — free to use, adapt, and share with attribution.

Explore it here: https://github.com/armourinfosec/Enterprise-Windows-Infrastructure-Security

If it's useful, a ⭐ on GitHub helps others find it. Feedback, issues, and pull requests are always welcome.

— The Armour Infosec Team
https://www.armourinfosec.com
